records.ety: [L. penuria; cf. Gr. <?/ hunger, <?/ poverty, need, <?/ one who works for his daily bread, a poor man, <?/ to work for one's daily bread, to be poor: cf. F. pnurie.]

defs: Absence of resources; want; privation; indigence; extreme poverty; destitution.
quotes: They were exposed to hardship and penury.
It arises in neither from penury of thought.
